focus: Automation authority, checkout-free conflict preflight, partitioning, and semantic-conflict quarantine
noveltyJustification: Six findings are fully new and one partially extends the prior orchestration treatment of unavoidable conflicts, yielding (6 + 0.5×1) / 7 = 0.929.
keyQuestions: Which automation surface and conflict-avoidance rules are reliable enough for unattended AI sessions?
answeredQuestions: Which automation surface and conflict-avoidance rules are reliable enough for unattended AI sessions?
ruledOut: Blind FIFO bypass without proven path and dependency independence

summary: A supervised publisher owns queue, journal, authentication, fetch, integration, push reconciliation, projection refresh, and crash recovery; hooks and wrappers only submit or bootstrap.
Client hooks are configurable, sometimes bypassable, and command-scoped; receive-side hooks or hosted branch protection enforce only the remote ref boundary.
merge-tree and diff-tree provide checkout-free merge/conflict and changed-path preflight against the exact current base.
Path reservations, per-session subtrees, and additive-only records reduce overlap but do not prove semantic independence; CODEOWNERS is review routing, not locking.
Conflicting transactions remain pinned and journaled outside shared/session worktrees; only proven-independent queue entries may bypass them.
